Notice Of Joinder And Joinder To Bayer Healthcare Pharmaceuticals Inc., Bayer Corporation, And Bayer Healthcare Llc'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-28-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT MERRY X-RAY CHEMICAL CORPORATION's Joinder To Bayer Healthcare Pharmaceuticals Inc., Bayer Corporation, And Bayer Healthcare Llc'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings against plaintiff Ulleseit is granted without leave to amend. Mr. Ulleseit's counsel withdrew from this case on June 20, 2023, after Mr. Ulleseit died. As his former counsel noted in his declaration in support of the motion to withdraw, decedent's estate has been given ample time to retain counsel and prosecute this case but it has not chosen to do so. Given the death and lack of any party to puruse this action, the court grants judgment on the pleadings without leave to amend in favor of defendant. (Est. of Edwards (1978) 82 Cal.App.3d 885, 893 [action cannot proceed where plaintiff dies and estate is not substituted into the case].)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Aug-31-2017 Continued To Nov-15-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

SPECIALLY APPEARING DEFENDANT BAYER PHARMA AG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS for lack of personal jurisdiction.

Defendant Bayer Pharma AG's motion to quash service of summons is continued to November 15, 2017 to allow plaintiffs Kathleen Geisse, Curtis Ulleseit, and Lisa Wehlmann to conduct discovery to support their positions that they properly served defendant Bayer Pharm AG and that this court may exercise specific personal jurisdiction over Bayer Pharma AG. The scope of permissible discovery is limited to the service of process and personal jurisdiction issues. While plaintiffs have not presented sufficient facts to show valid service of process or specific personal jurisdiction, there is a possibility that discovery may yield information for them to do so. Supplemental opposition papers must be filed and served no later than November 1, 2017 and supplemental reply papers must be filed and served no later than November 8, 2017. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)
